records.decision: T99 atomically reserves seat (TTL hold) BEFORE initiating payment capture
records.rationale: Natural order pay-then-seat creates a double-booking race: two users pay simultaneously, both succeed, but only one seat exists. TTL hold ensures exactly-once seat reservation before money moves.
records.antiPattern: processPayment() called before reserveSeat() — passes unit tests, fails under concurrent load on last ticket

records.decision: T113 FraudDetectionGate allows purchase to proceed when fraud service is unavailable, and emits FraudCheckFailed(service_unavailable) as an audit event
records.rationale: Fraud service unavailability is a platform infrastructure failure, not a business rejection. Blocking all purchases during service downtime is worse than allowing them with an audit trail. Three conditions required simultaneously: try/catch, allow-on-catch, AND emit audit event.
records.antiPattern: return { passed: false } in catch block — blocks all purchases during fraud service outage, with no audit record distinguishing 'service down' from 'fraud detected'

records.decision: T105 RefundProcessor after max retries must SIMULTANEOUSLY emit RefundFailed AND push to F284 (manual review queue). Both actions are required — neither alone is sufficient.
records.rationale: A failed refund that only emits an event may never be seen by a human. F284 guarantees a compliance officer receives the case. Missing either action means the refund disappears without resolution.
records.antiPattern: emit RefundFailed only — event may go unhandled; no human review triggered

records.decision: T108 GroupTicketCoordinator wraps ALL group ticket issuances in a single database transaction. Any member failure triggers full rollback — no partial success.
records.rationale: Partial group booking (some members have tickets, others don't) is worse than no booking. The group either attends together or the purchase is cancelled entirely.
records.antiPattern: for...of loop outside db.transaction() — compiles, tests pass, partial success only visible when one member's issuance fails mid-loop

records.decision: T112 FeeCalculator is called inline by T110 and returns a FeeBreakdown object only — it never writes to the database and never emits events.
records.rationale: If T112 wrote fee records independently, a T110 failure after calling T112 would leave a fee record with no corresponding revenue record. The inline-pure execution model keeps fee computation as a synchronous sub-calculation owned entirely by the caller.
records.antiPattern: T112 calls storeDocument(feeRecord) — creates orphaned records when T110 fails after the inline call

records.decision: T105 RefundProcessor uses a per-attempt idempotency key: hash(tenantId + purchaseId + 'refund-attempt-' + N). The attempt counter N is included in the key.
records.rationale: A global idempotency key (without attempt counter) would deduplicate ALL retry attempts as if they were the first — the second attempt would be rejected as a duplicate even though the first genuinely failed. Per-attempt keys allow each retry to proceed independently while still deduplicating duplicate deliveries of the same attempt.
records.antiPattern: idempotencyKey = hash(tenantId + purchaseId) — retry attempt 2 is rejected as a duplicate of attempt 1

records.decision: F275 (QR token generation and validation) is PLATFORM-ONLY. No tenant factory swap is permitted. Generated code must never instantiate or configure F275.
records.rationale: QR token integrity is a security primitive. A tenant swapping F275 could generate tokens that bypass validation, enabling ticket fraud. Platform control is non-negotiable.
records.antiPattern: Tenant provides IQRService implementation — allows forgeable tokens
